Context: This chunk from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' for April 2025 provides an overview of the Indian real estate market. It covers market sentiment, recent investments, policy updates, and performance indices across eight monitored cities.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 2

# Overview

### Realestonomics
Suraksha Group-controlled Jaypee Infratech will invest around INR 6,000 cr over the next three years to complete stalled housing projects, comprising 20,000 units, and assured homebuyers that it would adhere to the deadline promised in the approved resolution plan.

### Market sentiment
**Favourable**
(Scale: Favourable, Neutral, Unfavourable)

### What’s new
Aranya Real Estates Fund Advisors has invested INR 130 cr in a new residential project by Casagrand in South Bengaluru.

### Policy update
Haryana Chief minister Nayab Singh Saini has rejected the proposal of the revenue department to revise the circle rate. So now, the old circle rate will remain applicable. The circle rates were last increased by 10-30% in December 2024, and they were effective until March 2025.

### Market Indices
*   **Office Index:** Demand for office space increased in most cities
*   **Retail Index:** Retail space take-up in shopping malls increased in most cities
*   **Residential Index:** New launches went up in most cities

**Cities Monitored:**
*   Delhi NCR
*   Ahmedabad
*   Mumbai
*   Pune
*   Kolkata
*   Hyderabad
*   Bengaluru
*   Chennai

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

final_embedding_text:
Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor, April 2025" details the "Surging Land Deals in 2024" across India. It provides an overview of land acquisitions by developers, including total acreage, transaction value, and geographical distribution across various urban centers.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 3

# What’s trending in real estate
**Focus sector: Land and Capital Markets**

### Surging Land Deals in 2024

The year 2024 marked a watershed year in India's real estate sector, characterized by a surge in land acquisitions. Developers across the country embarked on an ambitious expansion drive, securing a vast 2,335 acres of land through 134 distinct transactions in 23 major urban centers. These strategic land acquisitions, valued at a staggering INR 39,742 crore, laid the foundation for potential development of 194 million sq. ft of real estate. While Tier I cities maintained their dominance, accounting for 72% of the land purchases, the year witnessed a significant shift towards smaller urban centers. Tier II and III cities claimed a substantial 28% share of the acquisitions, translating to 662 acres of land. This trend signals a growing recognition of the untapped potential in these emerging markets. Notably, cities like Nagpur, Varanasi, Indore, Vrindavan, and Ludhiana emerged as unexpected hotspots in this land acquisition spree. Their prominence in the year's transactions underscores a broader trend of geographical diversification in real estate development, moving beyond the traditional metropolitan strongholds.

The Mumbai Metropolitan Region (MMR) emerged as the frontrunner in land acquisition for 2024, with developers securing approximately 407 acres through 19 separate deals, accounting for 17% of the year's total land transactions. This represents a significant 41% increase from the previous year's 288.9 acres. Notable transactions included single deals of 50 acres or more in micro-markets such as Khalapur, Palghar, and Khapoli. While MMR led in terms of land area acquired, the National Capital Region (NCR) surpassed other cities in the number of deals closed, with 36 land transactions throughout the year. Within Delhi NCR, Gurugram saw the highest activity with 21 deals, followed by Noida with 14, and Ghaziabad with one.

81% of the land acquired during the year by developers was earmarked for proposed residential developments. This would translate into a massive development potential of 158 million sq. ft and cater to the ever-increasing housing demand in the country. Developers are banking on the continued homebuying interest in the residential sector as a top priority for augmenting their new supply pipeline.

**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS, India, JLL
samantak.das@jll.com

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

| Cities | Office rent | Retail rent | Residential capital value |

| Kolkata | Growing | Growing | Growing |

*   **Office leasing:** Increase
*   **Retail leasing:** Increase

Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor" for April 2025 provides a detailed overview of the real estate market in Ahmedabad. It covers sector updates for office, retail, and residential, including key transactions and launches, alongside market data on rental and capital values.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 5

# Ahmedabad

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Demand for office space remained stagnant.
    *   **Key transaction:** Indigo Airlines at Gift City
*   **Retail:** Muted demand in malls; Stagnant demand for high-streets.
    *   **Key transaction:** Kalyan Jewellers at Nikol
*   **Residential:** Sales and launches remain growth oriented.
    *   **Key launch:** The Skyon at Vastrapur

**Note:** No major transaction was recorded.

### Infrastructure/policy update
No major infrastructure or policy updates were recorded.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (sq ft/month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 40 - 60 |
| Retail | 120 - 150 |
| Residential | 20 - 30 |

*Caption: Rental value (sq ft/month) in Ahmedabad. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital Value (INR/sq ft) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 10,000 |
| Retail | 25,000 - 30,000 |
| Residential | 6,000 - 8,000 |

*Caption: Capital Value (INR/sq ft) in Ahmedabad. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
A riverfront plot near Ashram Road saw a significant increase in its development rights value. Over just three months, the price per sq m. at Vallabh Sadan jumped by INR 8,824.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: The 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report by JLL, published in April 2025 for India, provides a detailed overview of the real estate market. This specific section focuses on Bengaluru, detailing sector updates, infrastructure developments, market data for rental and capital values, and key city highlights.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 6

# Bengaluru

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Outer Ring Road and Whitefield continue to have the highest leasing traction.
    *   **Key transaction:** Toyo Modec at Indiranagar
*   **Retail:** Low vacancy levels in prominent malls resulting in lower traction.
    *   **Key transaction:** Volt at Hennur Road
*   **Residential:** Bannerghatta Road, Sarjapur Road in the south and Shettigere in north Bengaluru saw most of the launches.
    *   **Key launch:** Barca at Godrej MSR City at Shettigere

**Note:** Shriram Properties has acquired 5 acres of land parcel in Yelahanka to develop a residential project with a Gross Development Value (GDV) of INR 200-250 cr.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Bruhat Bengaluru Mahanagara Palike (BBMP) has extended the deadline for the 5% rebate on property tax for the financial year 2025-26 to May 31st 2025.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (INR/ sq ft/ month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 100 - 140 |
| Retail | 150 - 190 |
| Residential | 30 - 50 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/ sq ft/ month) in Bengaluru. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 12,000 - 16,000 |
| Retail | 18,000 - 22,000 |
| Residential | 12,000 - 16,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/ sq ft/ month) in Bengaluru. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
*   Brigade Group has partnered to develop a 20-acre residential plotted project in Malur.
*   Aranya Real Estates Fund Advisors has invested INR 130 cr in a new residential project by Casagrand in South Bengaluru.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: The 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report by JLL, published in April 2025, provides an overview of the real estate market dynamics across various Indian cities. This specific section focuses on Chennai, detailing sector updates, infrastructure developments, market data for rental and capital values, and city highlights for office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 7

# Chennai

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Sustained leasing momentum led by BFSI and Manufacturing firms in the SBD OMR, SBD & CBD micromarkets.
    *   **Key transaction:** TCS at OMR
*   **Retail:** Prominent high streets and malls led the leasing.
    *   **Key transaction:** Jaypore at RA Puram.
*   **Residential:** New launches and sales activity remain muted.
    *   **Key launch:** Chaitanya Providence at Nungambakkam

**Note:** Sify data center campus of INR 1,882 cr was inaugurated in Chennai by Tamil Nadu CM.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Phase III of Chennai Peripheral Ring Road (CPRR) project at Tiruvallur was inaugurated with an estimated cost of INR 2,700 cr.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 90 |
| Retail | 120 - 160 |
| Residential | 30 - 50 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) in Chennai. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 8,000 - 12,000 |
| Retail | 10,000 - 14,000 |
| Residential | 14,000 - 18,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) in Chennai. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
Dixon Technologies to set up INR 1,000 cr plant near Chennai.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: This chunk, extracted from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' for April 2025, provides a detailed overview of the real estate market in Delhi NCR. It covers sector-specific updates, infrastructure and policy changes, market data including rental and capital values, and key city highlights for the period.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 8

# Delhi NCR

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Office space take-up remained strong.
    *   **Key transaction:** Avanta Business Centre at Nehru Place
*   **Retail:** Healthy retail leasing activity across Delhi NCR.
    *   **Key transaction:** Maison Messika at Chanakyapuri
*   **Residential:** Launches and sales remain strong.
    *   **Key launch:** Trump Residences at Sector 69, Gurugram

**Note:** Suraksha Group-controlled Jaypee Infratech will invest around INR 6,000 cr over the next three years to complete stalled housing projects, comprising 20,000 units, and assured homebuyers that it would adhere to the deadline promised in the approved resolution plan.

### Infrastructure/policy update
Haryana Chief minister Nayab Singh Saini has rejected the proposal of the revenue department to revise the circle rate. So now, the old circle rate will remain applicable. The circle rates were last increased by 10-30% in December 2024, and they were effective until March 2025.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 80 - 240 |
| Retail | 200 - 280 |
| Residential | 30 - 60 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) in Delhi NCR. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 15,000 - 30,000 |
| Retail | 25,000 - 35,000 |
| Residential | 15,000 - 20,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) in Delhi NCR. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
*   Godrej Properties sold 275 homes worth over INR 2,000 cr at the launch of its luxury project, Godrej Riverine, in Sector 44, Noida. The Mumbai-based real-estate developer sold over 9 lac sq ft area in the project.
*   Trehan Iris has partnered with Sarovar Hotels to bring the Sarovar Portico brand to New Gurugram region. The hotel Iris Sarovar Portico is located at Iris Broadway Mall in Sector 85, Gurugram.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

*   Godrej Properties sold 275 homes worth over INR 2,000 cr at the launch of its luxury project, Godrej Riverine, in Sector 44, Noida. The Mumbai-based real-estate developer sold over 9 lac sq ft area in the project.
*   Trehan Iris has partnered with Sarovar Hotels to bring the Sarovar Portico brand to New Gurugram region. The hotel Iris Sarovar Portico is located at Iris Broadway Mall in Sector 85, Gurugram.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

It covers sector-specific updates, infrastructure and policy changes, market data including rental and capital values, and key city highlights for the period.

Context: The 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report by JLL, published in April 2025, provides an overview of the Indian real estate market. This specific section focuses on Hyderabad, detailing sector updates, market data, infrastructure developments, and city highlights for office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 9

# Hyderabad

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Leasing activity is restricted to Hitec City and Gachibowli.
    *   **Key transaction:** Wipro at Hitec City
*   **Retail:** Prime retail locations continue to experience robust leasing.
    *   **Key transaction:** PMJ jewellers at Panjagutta.
*   **Residential:** Property launches and sales continue at a steady pace.
    *   **Key launch:** Casagrand Belair at Dundigal.

**Note:** Marubeni Corporation signed a LoI with Telangana government to establish a state-of-the-art industrial park in 'Future City'. The project involves an initial investment of INR 1,000 cr and will be developed in phases on 600 acres.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The Central government has given initial approval for the Hyderabad-Amaravati Greenfield project, a 220-km six-lane expressway, after an 11-year wait since Andhra Pradesh's bifurcation. The Ministry of Home Affairs has directed MoRTH to prepare a detailed project report for this new expressway.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 60 - 90 |
| Retail | 120 - 160 |
| Residential | 20 - 40 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) in Hyderabad. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 6,000 - 9,000 |
| Retail | 12,000 - 16,000 |
| Residential | 6,000 - 9,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) in Hyderabad. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
UBS leased 1.5 mn sq ft at Phoenix Centaurus in the Financial District, Gachibowli.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: This chunk from JLL's "Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor, April 2025" provides a detailed real estate overview for Kolkata. It includes sector updates for Office, Retail, and Residential, along with market data on rental and capital values, infrastructure updates, and city highlights.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor | 10

# Kolkata

### Sector Updates
*   **Office:** Office market showed a constant demand in April.
    *   **Key transaction:** KPMG at Salt Lake
*   **Retail:** Shopping malls reflected moderate demand.
    *   **Key transaction:** Style Bazar at Belur
*   **Residential:** New launches remained strong across micro markets.
    *   **Key launch:** Urban Vista Ph-II at Rajarhat

**Note:** DLF Ltd entered into an agreement with Srijan Realty to sell DLF IT SEZ including a land parcel, spread across 25.9 acres for INR 693 cr.

### Infrastructure/policy update
The East-West Metro's last 2.6-km leg underwent the mandatory inspection by the Commission of Railway Safety (CRS). The commercial launch of the section will allow operations of the entire 16.6 km East-West Metro.

### Market Data
| Sector | Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 80 - 120 |
| Retail | 140 - 200 |
| Residential | 20 - 40 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) in Kolkata. Values shown in ranges.*

| Sector | Capital value (INR/sq ft) Range |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 10,000 - 14,000 |
| Retail | 16,000 - 22,000 |
| Residential | 12,000 - 16,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) in Kolkata. Values shown in ranges.*

### City highlights
Peerless Group has launched a mixed-use project Trayam including Residential, Office and Retail in New Town AAI.

© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: The 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report by JLL, published in April 2025, provides an overview of the Indian real estate market. This specific section focuses on Mumbai, detailing sector updates, key transactions, launches, rental and capital values, and infrastructure/policy changes for office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor

# Mumbai

### Office
SBD North emerged as the frontrunner in leasing velocity
**Key transaction:** ICICI Lombard General Insurance at Andheri

### Retail
Demand for malls and high streets improved.
**Key transaction:** The Souled Store at Mira Road

### Residential
Sales and launches remain robust.
**Key launch:** Aditya Paradiso at Andheri West.

---

Abhinandan Lodha's firm is set to invest INR 2,500 cr in three vertical development projects, strategically located in South Mumbai and North MMR.

---

### Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)*
| Sector | Value Range (Approx.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 170 - 200 |
| Retail | 220 - 260 |
| Residential | 70 - 90 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) for Mumbai. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.*

### Capital value (INR/sq ft)*
| Sector | Value Range (Approx.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 24,000 - 28,000 |
| Retail | 26,000 - 30,000 |
| Residential | 22,000 - 26,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) for Mumbai. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.*

---

### Infrastructure/policy update
Maharashtra hikes ready reckoner rates, Mumbai will see 3.4% hike.

---

### City highlights
Adani Group company Mah-Hill Properties buys plot worth INR 170 cr in South Mumbai

11
© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Context: This chunk is from JLL's 'Pulse: Monthly Real Estate Monitor' report for April 2025, providing a detailed overview of Pune's real estate market. It covers sector updates, market data, infrastructure developments, and city highlights across office, retail, and residential segments.

Chunk Content: Pulse: Monthly real estate monitor

# Pune

### Office
Leasing activity primarily driven by Co-working providers in the Suburbs and SBD submarkets.
**Key transaction:** Smartworks at Yerwada

### Retail
High streets across the city have seen strong leasing activity.
**Key transaction:** Style Union at Warje

### Residential
Sales and Launches remain stable.
**Key launch:** Lodha Altero at Wakad

---

Vibe Realty entered Pune market with acquisition of joint development spanning across 1.03 mn sq ft of saleable area with 750 units. The sales value is anticipated to exceed INR 650 cr.

---

### Rental value (INR/sq ft/month)*
| Sector | Value Range (Approx.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 70 - 90 |
| Retail | 120 - 150 |
| Residential | 30 - 40 |

*Caption: Rental value (INR/sq ft/month) for Pune. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.*

### Capital value (INR/sq ft)*
| Sector | Value Range (Approx.) |
| :--- | :--- |
| Office | 10,000 - 12,000 |
| Retail | 14,000 - 16,000 |
| Residential | 12,000 - 14,000 |

*Caption: Capital value (INR/sq ft) for Pune. *The above graphs show the values in ranges.*

---

### Infrastructure/policy update
PMRDA officials announced Metro Line 3 trials in September, metro line likely to open for public by March 2026.

---

### City highlights
Lodha Group, signed a joint development agreement worth INR 165.3 cr to develop a plot measuring 0.2 mn sq ft in Wakad.

12
© 2025 Jones Lang LaSalle IP, Inc. All rights reserved.

Chunk Content: # JLL SEE A BRIGHTER WAY

### Research author
**Dr. Samantak Das**
Chief Economist and Head Research & REIS
+91 22 6620 7575 / +91 98198 65516
samantak.das@jll.com

### About JLL
For over 200 years, JLL (NYSE: JLL), a leading global commercial real estate and investment management company, has helped clients buy, build, occupy, manage and invest in a variety of commercial, industrial, hotel, residential and retail properties. A Fortune 500® company with annual revenue of $23.4 billion and operations in over 80 countries around the world, our more than 112,000 employees bring the power of a global platform combined with local expertise. Driven by our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world, we help our clients, people and communities SEE A BRIGHTER WAY<sup>SM</sup>. JLL is the brand name, and a registered trademark, of Jones Lang LaSalle Incorporated. For further information, visit jll.com.
